Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Linear issues related to the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues with a query related to 'expense' or 'CategoryMatcher' or 'edge case' (or similar), or by filtering on project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', to find Dmitri's ticket ENG-212 ("Remaining edge case coverage -- policy config validation & malformed data handling", assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-20), Lena's own tickets ENG-205 ("Implement CategoryMatcher module (rules-based, keyword scoring)", assigned to Lena, state: todo, due 2026-02-13), ENG-214 ("[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config -- default template limits used instead of custom values", assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-18), ENG-215 ("[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config -- malformed JSON types causing skipped exclusion checks and amount validation", assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-18), and ENG-217 ("[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline -- remove silent fallback, add type enforcement", assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-20), Samira's ENG-211 ("Pilot Monitoring -- Vectral Systems & Mosaic Robotics", assigned to Samira, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-27), ENG-219 ("Define launch readiness checklist for features with automated financial decisions", assigned to Samira, state: todo, due 2026-03-21), and the unassigned epic ENG-204 ("Epic: Integration Testing & Edge Case Coverage", state: backlog, due 2026-03-20).
OE 2: Retrieve comments on Dmitri's ENG-212 ticket using linear_list_comments with issueId 'issue_edge_cases_backlog' to find Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests) detailing the six schema validation tests he implemented (test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback), and his note that remaining tasks for ENG-212 include edge case tests for categorization, handling vendor abbreviations, and distinguishing between internet setup and monthly configurations.
OE 3: Retrieve comments on the pilot monitoring ticket ENG-211 using linear_list_comments with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring' to find Samira's comment (comment_samira_pause_confirmed) listing the remediation plan: ENG-216 (comprehensive audit, Dmitri, due Wed 3/19), ENG-217 (strengthening the pipeline, Lena, due Thu 3/20), ENG-218 (validation of dashboard types, Anh, due Fri 3/21), ENG-212 (test suite for schema validation, Dmitri, due 3/27), ENG-219 (checklist for launch readiness, Samira, due Fri 3/21), with earliest possible re-enablement week of 3/30 dependent on all fixes and AM approval. Also find Elena's directive comment (comment_elena_directive) requiring: auto-approve/deny paused, comprehensive audit by Wed 3/19, pipeline corrections before reactivation, and a one-page status update by Friday 3/21.
OE 4: Search Slack with a query related to 'Okafor' or 'keyboard tray' or 'misclassification' or 'keyword' (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve conversation history from #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_history, to find Lena's own message (ts: 1775566020, channel C003, dated 2026-04-07) where she acknowledged the James Okafor keyboard tray misclassification as a keyword gap ('keyboard tray' and 'wrist rest' should be in the ergonomic keyword list) and stated "It's a simple fix, and I'll deploy it today."
OE 5: Search Slack with a query related to 'audit' or 'validation tests' or 'gate' or 'launch readiness' (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve conversation history from #engineering channel (C003) using conversations_history, to find Samira's message (ts: 1775307240, channel C003) confirming the launch readiness gate will include: (1) all validation tests passing, (2) configuration data checked against source, (3) AM approval with no exceptions for timeline pressure.
OE 6: Search emails with a query related to 'audit' or 'expense audit' or 'remediation' or '47 expenses' (or similar) using search_emails (note: these emails are in the SENT folder, so the agent may need to specify folder_name 'SENT' if the default INBOX search does not return results) to find Samira's audit summary email (email_samira_audit_summary, sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com/david.chen@moveops.com/chloe.vance@moveops.com) reporting: 47 expenses reviewed, 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections (all Vectral), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic), $885 financial exposure, and noting a categorization error where 'keyboard tray' was incorrectly classified as office_supplies instead of ergonomic. The audit email's remediation status section also shows the current timeline: auto-approve/deny disabled, audit complete, pipeline hardening on schedule, schema validation tests in progress, dashboard type validation in progress, launch readiness checklist being drafted. Also find Samira's earlier response plan email (email_samira_response_plan, sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com/david.chen@moveops.com/marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC chloe.vance@moveops.com/mina.hashimoto@moveops.com/emeka.diallo@moveops.com) with the detailed remediation plan and timelines for each workstream.
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to 'setup fee' or 'internet setup' or 'Kevin Tran' or 'false rejection' (or similar) using search_emails (note: this email is in the SENT folder, so the agent may need to specify folder_name 'SENT' if the default INBOX search does not return results) to find Mina's email (from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com, CC chloe.vance@moveops.com/david.chen@moveops.com) reporting two false rejection incidents at Vectral, including Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup fee being auto-denied because the system treated setup fees as regular monthly expenses against the $150 limit.
OE 8: Retrieve comments on Lena's ENG-205 ticket (issue_category_matcher, "Implement CategoryMatcher module (rules-based, keyword scoring)") using linear_list_comments with issueId 'issue_category_matcher' to find Lena's comment (comment_lena_exclusion_handling) about CategoryMatcher keyword prioritization for ergonomic vs furniture. Also retrieve comments on the integration testing epic ENG-204 (issue_integration_testing) using linear_list_comments with issueId 'issue_integration_testing' to find Dmitri's edge case test comment (comment_dmitri_edge_case_tests) listing specific categorization edge cases including 'Ergonomic keyboard tray -> ergonomic' and the internet setup fee + first month combined on same receipt.
OE 9: Retrieve Lena's own comment on ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) using linear_list_comments with issueId 'issue_p1_mosaic_malformed' to find comment_lena_deploy_verified where Lena noted: "Kevin Tran's internet setup fee remains a known edge case -- the system considers all internet expenses against the $150 monthly limit. The distinction between internet setup and monthly charges requires a separate ticket. I'll file that for later." This comment also states "Closing ENG-214. Now starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217)" -- confirming that both ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) are completed work, which informs the ticket updates in OE 15 and OE 16.
OE 10: Search Airtable records for James Okafor's stipend record using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with a query related to 'Okafor' or filtering on the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) to find his record (recStip000000019): Employee Name: James Okafor, Account: Vectral Systems, Expense Category: Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, Amount Requested: $82, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot -- double bug: miscategorized as office_supplies (should be ergonomic) AND used stale $50 default limit. Pending manual correction."
OE 11: Update Lena's Linear ticket ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening, "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline -- remove silent fallback, add type enforcement") using linear_update_issue with id 'issue_pipeline_hardening' to reflect its actual completed status -- config corrections deployed, Dmitri's schema validation tests incorporated into the deployment gate. The update should distinguish work that is genuinely done versus what was only planned.
OE 12: Update Lena's Linear ticket ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher, "Implement CategoryMatcher module (rules-based, keyword scoring)") using linear_update_issue with id 'issue_category_matcher' to reflect the current state the module is implemented and deployed, but the ergonomic keyword list has a known gap (missing 'keyboard tray' and 'wrist rest') that caused the James Okafor misclassification, and whether the keyword fix Lena promised on April 7 was deployed or remains pending.
OE 13: Add a comment on Dmitri's validation ticket ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog) using linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_edge_cases_backlog'. The comment should explain the categorization gap: the CategoryMatcher's ergonomic keyword list is missing 'keyboard tray' and 'wrist rest', which caused James Okafor's $82 keyboard tray to be misclassified as office_supplies instead of ergonomic. It should note that Lena identified this on April 7 and committed to deploying the fix, and clarify whether this fix has been deployed or still needs to be tracked as part of ENG-212's remaining edge case work.
OE 14: Send a message to Dmitri Volkov via Slack using conversations_add_message in #engineering channel (channel_id: C003), or via email using send_email from lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, asking whether his remaining edge case tests in ENG-212 address the internet setup-fee-versus-monthly-charge distinction (the issue where Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup fee was treated as a monthly expense against the $150 limit), or whether Lena needs to file a separate ticket for this categorization logic.
OE 15: Update Lena's Linear ticket ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config, "[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config -- default template limits used instead of custom values") using linear_update_issue with id 'issue_p1_vectral_config' to reflect its completed status. Lena's deploy verification comment on ENG-215 (comment_lena_deploy_verified) explicitly states "Closing ENG-214", and the Vectral config corrections (replacing incorrect default template limits with the correct custom values from Chloe's spreadsheet -- the ingestion pipeline had silently fallen back to defaults after a parse error) have been deployed and verified. The ticket currently shows state in_progress and should be moved to done.
OE 16: Update Lena's Linear ticket ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, "[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config -- malformed JSON types causing skipped exclusion checks and amount validation") using linear_update_issue with id 'issue_p1_mosaic_malformed' to reflect its completed status. Lena's deploy verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirms config corrections deployed and verified in production, with all test cases passing (Danielle Osei's chair correctly approved, Andre Washington's desk correctly denied, coworking correctly denied). The ticket currently shows state in_progress and should be moved to done.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search for Linear issues related to the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues with a query related to 'expense' or 'CategoryMatcher' or 'edge case' (or similar), or by filtering on project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', to find Dmitri's ticket ENG-212 ("Remaining edge case coverage -- policy config validation & malformed data handling", assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-20), Lena's own tickets ENG-205 ("Implement CategoryMatcher module (rules-based, keyword scoring)", assigned to Lena, state: todo, due 2026-02-13), ENG-214 ("[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config -- default template limits used instead of custom values", assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-18), ENG-215 ("[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config -- malformed JSON types causing skipped exclusion checks and amount validation", assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-18), and ENG-217 ("[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline -- remove silent fallback, add type enforcement", assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-20), Samira's ENG-211 ("Pilot Monitoring -- Vectral Systems & Mosaic Robotics", assigned to Samira, state: in_progress, due 2026-03-27), ENG-219 ("Define launch readiness checklist for features with automated financial decisions", assigned to Samira, state: todo, due 2026-03-21), and the unassigned epic ENG-204 ("Epic: Integration Testing & Edge Case Coverage", state: backlog, due 2026-03-20).

OE 15: Update Lena's Linear ticket ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config, "[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config -- default template limits used instead of custom values") using linear_update_issue with id 'issue_p1_vectral_config' to reflect its completed status. The ticket currently shows state in_progress and should be moved to done.
OE 16: Update Lena's Linear ticket ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, "[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config -- malformed JSON types causing skipped exclusion checks and amount validation") using linear_update_issue with id 'issue_p1_mosaic_malformed' to reflect its completed status. The ticket currently shows state in_progress and should be moved to done.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate

✓Run #3The final response clearly states ENG-205 is implemented but the keyword fix ('keyboard tray' and 'wrist rest') was never deployed, and marks it as 'not gate-ready.' This distinguishes completed from planned work.
✓Run #4The final response clearly states that ENG-205 was moved to In Progress, that the keyword fix ('keyboard tray', 'wrist rest') was 'never deployed,' and that 'ENG-205 was still in Todo state.' This distinguishes the planned work from completed work.
✓Run #5The final response clearly states: 'The Okafor keyword fix was never confirmed deployed. ENG-205 was left in todo state with no follow-up. There's no deployment record.' The ticket was moved to In Progress to reflect partial completion with the keyword gap as pending work.
✗Run #6The response mentions 'keyboard tray' as missing from the ergonomic keyword list but never mentions 'wrist rest.' The Slack search results (tool_call_result_9.json) contained Lena's message explicitly saying both 'keyboard tray' and 'wrist rest' should be added, so the agent had access to this information but omitted 'wrist rest' from its response and ticket comment.
17 The final response identifies the audit statistics from Samira's email to Elena: 47 expenses reviewed, 83% accuracy (39 correct), and $885 total financial exposure.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The final response states: 'Full audit of all 47 expenses: 39 correct, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic). Financial exposure: $885.' All three key audit statistics are present.
✗Run #2The final response mentions 47 expenses and 83% accuracy (39 correct), but does NOT mention the $885 total financial exposure figure. The agent never found Samira's email to Elena (email searches returned no relevant results), so this data point was missing.
✓Run #3The final response includes: '47 expenses audited. 39 correct, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic). $885 total financial exposure.' All three required audit statistics are present.
✓Run #4The final response states: '47 expenses reviewed → 39 correct, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic). Financial exposure: $885.' All three required statistics are present (47 expenses, 39/83% correct, $885 exposure).
✓Run #5The final response states '47 expenses reviewed — 39 correct, 5 false rejections (Vectral), 3 false approvals (Mosaic), $885 total financial exposure.' All three key figures are present: 47 expenses, 39 correct (83% implied), and $885. These match the email_samira_audit_summary which was retrieved via the search_emails SENT folder call.
✗Run #6The response mentions 47 expenses and $885 financial exposure but never explicitly states '83% accuracy' or '39 correct.' More critically, all email searches returned empty results, so the agent never found Samira's email to Elena — the data came from Dmitri's Slack message, not the email. The criterion specifically requires data from Samira's email to Elena.
18 The final response identifies the completed remediation actions from Samira's email to Elena: auto-approve/deny has been disabled and the audit of all 47 pilot expenses is complete.
outcome

The agent searched for the email with multiple queries but the search didn't return it. The response only says 'She sent the audit summary to Elena' without detailing its contents.
✗Run #2The agent never found Samira's audit summary email to Elena — all email searches returned empty results. The agent did not surface from an email that auto-approve/deny was disabled and the audit of all 47 expenses was complete as Samira-to-Elena communication. The Samira remediation message found was a Slack post, not the email to Elena.
✗Run #3The final response does not explicitly identify the two completed remediation actions from Samira's email to Elena: auto-approve/deny disabled and audit of all 47 pilot expenses completed. The agent summarized audit findings but didn't surface these as completed remediation checkboxes from the email.
✗Run #4The final response does not explicitly mention the two completed remediation actions from Samira's email/communications to Elena: (1) auto-approve/deny has been disabled and (2) the audit of all 47 pilot expenses is complete. The response references the audit stats but doesn't frame these as completed remediation actions from Samira's update to Elena.
✗Run #5The final response's 'What Samira Told Elena' section only discusses the keyword gap finding. It does not identify the completed remediation actions from Samira's email to Elena: that auto-approve/deny was disabled (completed Tuesday) and that the audit of all 47 pilot expenses was complete. These were explicitly listed as ✅ completed items in email_samira_audit_summary.
✗Run #6The response does not mention that auto-approve/deny has been disabled or that the audit of all 47 pilot expenses is complete as completed remediation actions from Samira's email to Elena. All email searches returned empty, so the agent never found Samira's email to Elena containing this information.

Anh's dashboard type validation work is also not mentioned in the agent's final response.

It omits two of the four in-progress workstreams from the email: dashboard type validation (Anh) and launch readiness checklist (Samira). ✗Run #6The response does not identify the specific in-progress remediation workstreams from Samira's email to Elena: Anh's dashboard type validation and Samira's launch readiness checklist being drafted are absent. All email searches returned empty results, so the agent never found Samira's email to Elena with these details.
20 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to find and retrieve ExpenseBot-related tickets including ENG-212, ENG-205, ENG-217, ENG-219, and ENG-211.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'categorization', 'CategoryMatcher', 'validation', 'edge case', 'launch readiness', 'assignee: moveops_lena_bjorkstrom', finding tickets ENG-212, ENG-205, ENG-217, ENG-219, and ENG-214/215.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries such as 'categorization', 'CategoryMatcher', 'validation', 'keyword', and 'Okafor', and also filtered by assignee (Dmitri Volkov). This found ENG-212, ENG-205, ENG-217, ENG-214, ENG-215, and related tickets.
✓Run #3The agent used linear_list_issues with queries 'CategoryMatcher', 'categorization edge case', 'Okafor misclassification', 'launch readiness', 'schema tests', 'validation', 'keyword', and also filtered by project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer' and by assignee. This clearly covers the required tool selection.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows multiple linear_list_issues calls with queries including 'categorization', 'CategoryMatcher', 'edge case', 'validation', and 'Okafor', plus linear_get_issue calls for issue_category_matcher, issue_pipeline_hardening, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, and issue_p1_vectral_config. Linear was directly queried.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows multiple linear_list_issues calls: by assignee (moveops_dmitri_volkov, moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), by query ('validation' with project filter, 'keyword', 'launch readiness'), all returning results about the ExpenseBot tickets ENG-212, ENG-205, ENG-217, ENG-219, etc.
✓Run #6The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls with queries including 'categorization', 'CategoryMatcher', 'validation', 'edge case', 'keyword', 'launch readiness', 'setup fee', and 'internet recurring', successfully retrieving ExpenseBot-related tickets including ENG-212, ENG-205, ENG-217, ENG-219.

query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
